what_it_does: This file is the root React shell for the company canvas application. It mounts a tldraw board, instantiates a shared application controller via useAppController, distributes it through AppContext, and renders a responsive CSS-grid chrome shell over the canvas. It also implements a top-level workspace switcher among 'canvas', 'review', and 'lattice'; phone-specific top and bottom navigation; per-panel error boundaries; and small surface affordances for annotating and recording ui:// click journeys.
observations: The file opens with a large inline changelog comment describing the F0 carve from a 1659-line monolithic Hud into multiple files.

It imports many regions and components (Toolbar, Palette, Inspector, Inbox, Grow, OpPanels, Activity, RhmChat, ClaudeChat, GreetingCard, MobileTray, Walkthrough, Workshop, Settings, Fleet, CognitionView, Review, LatticeView, etc.).
Hud uses useEditor and useAppController, then wraps everything in AppContext.Provider.
A view state ('canvas' | 'review' | 'lattice') selects which full-workspace surface renders.
MobileTopBar is rendered outside the grid shell so it persists across all views.
The app-shell div uses pointer-events passthrough for the canvas cell and re-enables pointer-events on chrome cells.
Numerous in-canvas overlays are wrapped in PanelErrorBoundary.
Deep-link useEffect reads ?review, ?mockup, and ?guide URL parameters.
Another useEffect mirrors the current view to document.body.dataset.view for CSS.
A useEffect auto-raises the mobile 'rhm' sheet when ctrl.proposal?.interactive becomes true.
App configures Tldraw with shape utils NodeShapeUtil and ForagerShapeUtil, persistenceKey 'company-canvas-v4', and nullifies StylePanel/ActionsMenu/QuickActions.
AnnotateBar and JourneyBar are small, conditionally rendered controls built on the .rhm-indicating style vocabulary.
standouts: Extensive inline design-rationale comments are treated almost like a changelog.
A three-workspace view switch (canvas / review / lattice) is implemented without a router.
The grid shell is layered over the live tldraw board with pointer-events:none on the canvas cell.
Defense-in-depth error boundaries: a shell-level boundary plus per-panel boundaries.
Mobile-specific chrome is explicitly separated and survives view switches.
Small addressable-UI affordances: annotate-mode comment bar and reverse journey recording/replay.
conventions: React functional components with hooks (useState, useEffect).
Context provider pattern for distributing controller state.
CSS-grid layout with named areas and pointer-events toggling.
Per-panel error boundaries for graceful degradation.
data-ui-ref attributes to make DOM elements addressable.
Imports grouped by origin (tldraw, local regions, local components, api).
concerns: MobileTopBar casts useApp() result to any, losing type safety.
Multiple eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps comments suppress dependency warnings.
Inline arrow functions in JSX event handlers may cause unnecessary re-renders.
AnnotateBar's submit function is recreated each render and closes over local state.
JourneyBar uses defaultValue on a select and mutates e.currentTarget.value directly.
The file mixes layout, view routing, mobile chrome, and small feature components, making it a broad top-level orchestrator.
Many behavior claims are in comments and may drift from the actual controller implementation.
notes: Top-level orchestrator that hosts tldraw, distributes shared state, and arranges responsive chrome plus canvas/review/lattice workspaces.
questions: What is the exact shape and contract of the object returned by useAppController?
How does AppContext expose its controller (what fields/methods does useApp provide)?
What is the implementation of useAppController and how are ctrl.edges, ctrl.proposal, ctrl.annotateLocus, ctrl.replayJourney, etc. derived?
How do the imported region components consume the controller?
What is the ForagerShapeUtil and how do forager shapes coexist with node shapes?
How does the backend API surface support /api/annotate, /api/journeys, and the review/lattice endpoints?
What is the 'builder' mobile tab content and how does setMobileTab('builder') interact with LatticeView?
How are settings opened and what does the Settings modal render?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide a restructured, grid-based layout shell replacing fixed-absolute islands; the file does exactly that while also adding view switching, mobile navigation, error-boundary defense, and small addressable-UI affordances.
apparent_themes: Separation of operating canvas from review/lattice workspaces
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a stable, always-live control surface for an AI-assisted operations canvas: keep the tldraw board running underneath, make every UI element addressable and recordable, unify desktop and mobile experiences, and prevent any single panel failure from killing the whole surface.
novelty: Dense changelog-style inline comments; annotate and reverse-journey affordances tied to indicated ui:// elements; a three-workspace switch without a router; grid-over-canvas with pointer-events passthrough; auto-raising mobile sheets based on interactive proposals.
contribution: This file contributes the top-level composition architecture: the tldraw host, the controller context distribution point, the responsive grid shell, the workspace switcher, the mobile navigation bars, the shell-level error boundary, and the wiring surface where all other regions and panels are mounted.
summary_for_embedding: App.tsx is the root React shell for the company canvas, mounting a tldraw board, distributing application state via AppContext, laying out responsive chrome regions over a pointer-events-transparent canvas, and switching between canvas, review, and lattice workspaces.
intention_for_embedding: To replace a monolithic HUD with a maintainable, mobile-aware, error-resilient layout shell that keeps the operating canvas live while surfacing chat, builder, inspector, and alternate workspaces.

symbols.does: Renders a phone-only top bar with status, tools sheet, and mode selector.
symbols.description: MobileTopBar is a mobile-specific chrome component that displays the current presence or pending count, toggles a tools sheet containing the view switch and Toolbar, and lets the operator change mode via a select. It is rendered outside the main app-shell so it remains visible across canvas, review, and lattice views.

symbols.does: Shows a comment input when an indicated ui:// element is in annotate click mode.
symbols.description: AnnotateBar conditionally renders a small comment bar in the .rhm-indicating style. It binds a local text input and, on Enter or button click, calls ctrl.annotateLocus to POST a comment at the currently indicated address. It only appears when clickMode(indicated) returns 'annotate'.

symbols.does: Validates and submits the annotate-bar comment text.
symbols.description: submit is the nested async helper inside AnnotateBar. It trims the input, aborts if empty, invokes annotateLocus, and clears the text field. It is the concrete handler that turns the annotate affordance into an API call.

symbols.does: Toggles recording of ordered ui:// click-paths and replays selected journeys.
symbols.description: JourneyBar provides record/stop and replay controls using the .rhm-indicating style. It fetches the list of recorded journeys when recording finalizes and calls toggleJourneyRecording and replayJourney from the controller to capture navigation paths and step the view through their addresses.

symbols.does: Builds the controller, provides it via AppContext, and lays out the responsive shell plus workspace switcher.
symbols.description: Hud uses useEditor and useAppController to create a shared controller, wraps descendants in AppContext.Provider, manages view state (canvas/review/lattice) and mobile tab state, initializes deep-link parameters, mirrors the view to document.body.dataset.view, auto-raises the RHM sheet on interactive proposals, and renders the grid shell with all regions, error boundaries, modals, and the mobile top bar.

symbols.does: Mounts the tldraw board with custom shape utils and suppressed default panels, nesting Hud inside.
symbols.description: App is the default-export root component. It renders a full-viewport Tldraw instance configured with NodeShapeUtil and ForagerShapeUtil, a persistence key of 'company-canvas-v4', nullified default tldraw chrome panels, and the Hud component as its child so the controller and editor share the same tldraw instance.
